concept_name: EMF — the Energy a Cell Gives Each Charge
source_book: NCERT Class 12 Physics, Ch.3 Current Electricity (§3.11 Electromotive Force) — table-of-contents + Indian-anchor reference only; teaching authored from first principles. DC Pandey consulted for chapter SCOPE only (Current Electricity ToC: emf / cells / internal resistance / grouping-of-cells sections) to confirm emf's definition is a distinct sub-topic ahead of internal resistance and cell grouping — no teaching method, example problem, or figure imported. HC Verma consulted for the historical SCOPE of the electromotive-'force' misnomer (§32.5) only — confirms it is a genuine, chapter-standard misconception worth confronting, never its phrasing or worked examples. The atomic idea: a cell is a charge pump whose chemistry does work W on charge q lifting it from the low (−) terminal to the high (+) terminal; emf ε = W/q, units volts = joules per coulomb — an intensive property of the cell's chemistry, fixed and independent of the circuit, never a force. With no current flowing, an ideal voltmeter across the terminals reads exactly ε — the measurement condition that bridges to the next diamond. SCOPE: this is the FIFTH Ch.3 concept, continuous with drift_velocity, ohms_law, resistivity, and combination_of_resistors (all SHIPPED) — same particle_field (2D p5.js) renderer family, a new dedicated circuit scenario (charge-pump cell + potential-energy ladder + terminal voltmeter). Diamond 1 of a two-diamond split — this cell is IDEAL (internal resistance r = 0); terminal voltage equals ε identically, open or closed, and NO on-screen statement claims otherwise. Does NOT cover internal resistance or the terminal-voltage droop V = ε − Ir (deferred to internal_resistance, Diamond 2, which reuses this exact cell + ladder), combinations of cells (deferred to combination_of_cells), Kirchhoff's rules, or the electrochemistry of electrode reactions (outside atomic scope — the mechanism shown here is the potential-energy ladder, not the chemistry itself). Renderer-reality note: this concept does NOT drive i/V_terminal/W via physics_engine_config.formulas/PM_interpolate — particle_field_renderer.ts computes them natively in engine JS via the shipped emf adapter (cEmf/cLoadR/emfSwitchOpen/emfCurrents), reading slider_controls.{emf,R,switch}; physics_engine_config below is authored as documentation per the Zod requirement, mirroring combination_of_resistors' convention.

real_world_anchor.primary: The emf hierarchy of real Indian cells: a 1.5 V Eveready dry cell in a torch, a 2 V lead-acid cell inside an inverter bank, a 3.7 V lithium-ion cell in every phone, a 12 V car battery — four everyday objects, four different numbers, and the number means one exact thing: how many joules that cell's chemistry hands every coulomb that passes through it. A dry cell the size of a thumb and a fat D-cell both say 1.5 V, because the chemistry is the same — the chemistry sets ε; the circuit never does.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Every student has read these numbers off real batteries their whole life without knowing what the number promises; 'define emf and state its SI unit' and 'distinguish emf from terminal potential difference' are board staples, and this hierarchy makes the definition concrete four times over.

emf.name: electromotive force (epsilon)
emf.role: slider — live ONLY in S4 (taught variable) and S6 (explore); locked to 1.5 on S1/S2/S3/S5 — reorder-safe per Rule 25d

R.name: load resistance
R.role: slider — live ONLY in S6; locked to 1.5 on S1-S5 (never the taught variable in this diamond)

switch.name: circuit switch (0=open, 1=closed)
switch.role: slider (2-state toggle) — live ONLY in S6; closed on S1-S4, forced open by open_circuit on S5; rendered as text (Open/Closed), never raw 0/1

q.name: charge in the traced packet (display-only, NOT an engine slider)
q.role: S3 ONLY — driven by the charge_double flag on the state clock (1 C → 2 C); NOT a control on any state

i.name: loop current
i.role: live readout — the ammeter; dies to 0 on S5

V_terminal.name: terminal voltage
V_terminal.role: IDEAL cell only (r=0) — always equals ε, open or closed; the voltmeter reading. Diamond 2 replaces with ε − i·r

W.name: work done by the cell on the traced packet
W.role: readout on the lifted packet — 1.5 J at q=1, 3.0 J at q=2 (S3)

W_per_q.name: work per unit charge — the definition of emf
W_per_q.role: the ladder step-height label; reads the SAME number as the emf badge at every instant, incl. during S3 doubling — this identity IS the aha

constraints: ε = W/q at every instant — an intensive property; doubling q doubles W but leaves ε (=W/q) unchanged (the S3 aha)
ε is set by the cell's chemistry — independent of R, switch state, and i (S4: the circuit never sets ε)
IDEAL cell (r=0): V_terminal = ε identically, open OR closed — NO droop authored; Diamond 2 replaces this with V = ε − ir
open circuit (switch=0) ⇒ i=0 exactly; and i=0 ⇒ V_terminal=ε is TRUE FOR ANY CELL (no current ⇒ no i·r term) — why S5's claim safely survives into Diamond 2, unlike a closed-circuit V=ε claim
i ≥ 0 always; emf ∈ [1,12], R ∈ [0.5,12] both > 0 ⇒ i = emf/R has no divide-by-zero in range
1 V ≡ 1 J/C exactly — the SI definition of the volt, not an approximation

aha_moment.statement: Double the charge doubles the work — but the step per coulomb never changes.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: The work readout climbs from 1.5 J to 3.0 J as the charge packet doubles, while the ladder's step height holds at 1.5 J/C the entire time.

STATE_1.title: The cell does work: every charge gets lifted

scene_composition.text: Inside the cell, every charge is lifted from the − terminal to the + terminal.
− is low potential, + is high potential — the pump raises every charge that passes through.
The ammeter reads i = 1.0 A — this is a normal, working loop.

tts_sentences.text_en: These drifting charges are the current I you already know — but the cell keeps spending energy to push them around this loop.
Inside, each charge is lifted from the low end to the high end — real work W, not just drift.

STATE_2.title: How big is the lift: ε = W/q

scene_composition.text: Step up = ε = 1.5 J/C — the measured size of the lift.
Flat along the wire — no potential is spent just travelling.
Equal drop across the load, R = 1.5 Ω — the same energy spent as heat.

tts_sentences.text_en: That lift has a real size: the ladder traces it as a step up of one point five joules for every coulomb.
The charge rides flat along the wire, then drops that same amount crossing the load.
That is the definition: emf epsilon equals work W divided by charge q.

STATE_3.title: Per charge, not a force — the primary aha

scene_composition.text: Work done: W = 1.5 J → 3.0 J — the total work doubles.
The step height holds at 1.5 J/C — per charge, never total.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here's the test: watch the charge packet double from one coulomb to two.
The total work W the cell does doubles — one point five joules becomes three — but the ladder step itself never moves.
That's the proof: emf is energy per charge, not a force — one volt really is one joule per coulomb, never newtons.

misconception_watch.belief: emf is a force that pushes the charges — electromotive FORCE.
misconception_watch.visual_counter: The units on screen are volts = joules per coulomb, and when the charge count doubles the total work W doubles while the ladder step height ε visibly holds — an intensive per-charge energy, not a force that would scale with the amount of charge.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: emf is the energy the cell hands each coulomb — measured in volts, never newtons.

scene_composition.text: 1.5 V dry cell · 2 V lead-acid cell.
3.7 V Li-ion cell · 12 V car battery.
Taller step = bigger emf epsilon — the chemistry decides, never the circuit.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag the emf slider and watch the lift itself grow — different cells, different epsilon, same law: current i equals epsilon over R.
A torch cell sits at one point five volts, a car battery at twelve — the chemistry inside sets that number, never the circuit outside.

STATE_5.title: Measuring emf: no current, full ε

scene_composition.text: Switch open — the loop is broken.
Ammeter at zero — no current flowing.
Voltmeter settles at V = ε = 1.5 V.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now open the switch: the loop breaks, beads coast to a stop, and the ammeter dies to zero.
With no current at all, the voltmeter across the terminals settles at the full emf epsilon — one point five volts, exactly.
That's how emf is measured — a real cell has more to tell once current flows again.

STATE_6.title: Explore: i = ε/R, every dial live

scene_composition.text: i = ε / R — every dial is live now.
Pump, ladder, ammeter, and voltmeter all respond live as you drag.
Drag epsilon, the load R, or flip the switch.

tts_sentences.text_en: Every dial is yours now — epsilon, load R, and the switch — the same law, i equals epsilon over R.

regeneration_variants.label: A rooftop water pump lifting bucket after bucket to a fixed height
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A motor pumps water from a ground tank up to a fixed rooftop tank. Every bucket of water it lifts gets the SAME amount of energy spent on it, set by how strong the motor is — not by how many buckets you send up.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: Energy spent per unit quantity moved is a fixed, intensive property of the pump (like ε = W/q for a cell) — doubling the number of buckets doubles the total work, but the energy-per-bucket never changes.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Optional supplementary anchor for STATE_2/STATE_3; the primary teaching anchor stays the four-cell emf hierarchy (dry cell/lead-acid/Li-ion/car battery).

regeneration_variants.label: A hand-crank torch/phone charger — harder cranking, bigger emf
regeneration_variants.physical_world: A hand-crank generator charges a phone: cranking harder or using a bigger internal magnet raises the emf it delivers per unit charge, exactly the way a different cell chemistry sets a different ε — the mechanism inside decides the number, not how the charging cable is wired.
regeneration_variants.same_physics: The source's own internal design (chemistry for a cell, crank/magnet strength for a generator) fixes the energy handed to every unit of charge — the external circuit never sets it.
regeneration_variants.scene_hint: Optional supplementary anchor for STATE_4's chemistry-sets-ε teaching.
